courses.title: Our
courses.subtitle: Choose the program that fits your career goals

items.title: Advanced Laptop Chip Level Course
items.description: Theory + Live Practical Workshop by Laptian Technical Institute, PCMC
highlights: Laptop motherboard architecture and power section analysis
Schematic reading, tracing, and board view interpretation
Component testing, fault diagnosis, and dead board repair
Advanced soldering techniques, BGA rework, and tools handling
items.detailedDescription: This is an advanced chip level laptop repair training program designed to transform you into a skilled and confident laptop motherboard repair professional. You will learn practical, step-by-step voltage rail analysis, signal tracing, and logical fault diagnosis methods used by industry experts. The course focuses on accuracy, productivity, and real repair outcomes—helping you work smarter and grow faster in your career. By the end of this laptop repair course in Pimpri and Chinchwad, Pune, you will think and troubleshoot like a professional chip-level technician.
whoShouldEnroll: Beginners starting their laptop repair journey
Job seekers looking for technical skills
Working technicians aiming to upgrade to chip-level repair
Entrepreneurs planning to start or expand a repair business
items.practicalTraining: Experience the most comprehensive hands-on training available. Work with over 50 different laptop models, handle real customer repairs during your internship, and learn business aspects including customer service, pricing strategies, and workshop management. Our 95% placement rate speaks to the quality and depth of this program.

curriculum.title: Analog & Digital Electronic Components
curriculum.description: Understand and work with ICs, chipsets, MOSFETs, transistors, capacitors, coils, and resistors used in laptop motherboards.

curriculum.title: Schematic & Board View Interpretation
curriculum.description: Learn to use schematics and board views to trace faults quickly and perform accurate motherboard repairs across different laptop brands.

curriculum.title: Dead Laptop & Short Circuit Repair
curriculum.description: Diagnose and fix short circuits using advanced tools and smart techniques applicable to laptops, desktops, tablets, and other electronic devices.

curriculum.title: Expert SMD & Micro-Soldering Techniques
curriculum.description: Master hot air stations, SMD tools, and micro-soldering methods with precision heat control and component-level accuracy.

curriculum.title: BIOS Programming & Editing
curriculum.description: Gain hands-on experience in BIOS recovery, SPI chip handling, and flashing techniques to fix corrupted firmware, resolve boot issues, and restore system stability using professional tools.

curriculum.title: Power Section & Signal Communication Analysis
curriculum.description: Diagnose charging circuits, standby rails, RAM rails, VRMs, and DC-DC converters, and analyze signal communication between CPU, RAM, BIOS, PCH, SIO, and peripherals to identify deep-level motherboard faults.

curriculum.title: Hands-On Training with Advanced Diagnostic Tools
curriculum.description: Work with professional repair lab tools to measure signals, track power flow, detect faults, and perform accurate chip-level motherboard repairs.

courseContent.title: Module 1: Basic Electronics & Circuit Fundamentals
courseContent.description: Study of basic circuit elements, passive components, semiconductor devices, digital electronics, and fault finding using diagnostic tools.
topics: Basic circuit elements and passive components (Resistor, Capacitor, Inductor, Diode)
Semiconductor devices: BJT, FET and MOSFET
Identification and testing of ICs and chipsets
Types of circuits: Series, Parallel, Open and Short circuits
Difference between analog and digital electronics
Logic gates: AND, NAND, OR, NOT and NOR
Fault finding using Digital Multimeter, SMD Tester and DSO/CRO

courseContent.title: Module 2: PS_ID, AD_ID & Laptop Battery Architecture
courseContent.description: Understanding adapter identification circuits, battery architecture, communication lines, and troubleshooting battery-related issues.
topics: Repair techniques for PS_ID and AD_ID
Laptop battery basics and different architectures

courseContent.title: Module 3: DCIN & Charging Section
courseContent.description: Comprehensive study of DC input and charging circuits across different motherboard architectures with tracing and troubleshooting methods.
topics: DCIN/VIN section in different architecture motherboards
Common DCIN faults and troubleshooting techniques
Charging section in different architecture boards
Charging related problems and troubleshooting techniques

courseContent.title: Module 4: 3V & 5V Step-Down Power Section
courseContent.description: Understanding always-on voltages, step-down converters, DC-DC circuits, and troubleshooting 3V/5V power rails.
topics: Difference between always-ON and suspend voltages
Controller functions and IC pin details

courseContent.title: Module 5: RAM Power & PWM Section
courseContent.description: Detailed study of RAM power rails, PWM controllers, supply sequences, and RAM communication with CPU.
topics: Supply sequence with power states (S5, S3, S0)
Voltage and signal requirements of DDR3, DDR3L, DDR4 and DDR5
RAM PWM faults and troubleshooting techniques
RAM slot common faults and solutions

courseContent.title: Module 6: Super IO (SIO/EC) Controller
courseContent.description: Understanding Super IO controller logic, triggering sequence, communication with system components, and troubleshooting.
topics: SIO voltage requirements and triggering logic
SIO/EC interaction with charging, PCH, RAM and step-down sections
Keyboard, touchpad and fan control role of SIO
SIO fault finding and troubleshooting techniques

courseContent.title: Module 7: RTC Section & No Power Diagnosis
courseContent.description: Study of RTC circuit, clock generation, reset signals, and troubleshooting no power and no trigger issues.
topics: SIO and PCH 32.768 kHz frequency generation
RTCRST, SRTCRST, SUSCLK and INTVRMEN concepts
No power and no trigger troubleshooting

courseContent.title: Module 8: VRM & CPU Core Section
courseContent.description: Understanding CPU power sequence, core voltage generation, VRM controller operation, and core fault diagnosis.
topics: Core IC requirements and pin functions
CPU power sequence and enable signals
CPU, PCH, BIOS and RAM SMBus communication
CPU and core faults diagnosis and troubleshooting

courseContent.title: Module 9: PCH Power & Signal Management
courseContent.description: In-depth analysis of PCH power signals, system resets, and communication with major motherboard components.
topics: Power management signals (RSMRST, SLP_S3, SLP_S4, SLP_S5, DPWROK, SYS_RESET)
PWROK, PWRBTN, SUSCLK, BATLOW and PLTRST signals
PCH communication with SIO, BIOS and CPU
Troubleshooting sound, USB, LAN, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and card reader issues

courseContent.title: Module 10: Display Section (LVDS & eDP)
courseContent.description: Understanding laptop display interfaces, signal tracing, and troubleshooting display-related faults.
topics: LVDS and eDP fault diagnosis and solutions
External display working but internal display not working

courseContent.title: Module 11: BIOS Programming & Signal Analysis
courseContent.description: BIOS programming, signal testing using DSO/CRO, ME region editing, and solving display and boot-related issues.
topics: BIOS update and fault solving techniques
BIOS waveform analysis for display faults
Diagnosing no display, dim display and delayed display issues
Noise and glitch detection using DSO

courseContent.title: Module 12: Power Sequence & Advanced Diagnostics
courseContent.description: Comprehensive study of power states, short detection, live case studies, and motherboard analysis using boardview.
topics: Power sequence with states S5, S4, S3 and S0
Identification of power rail short and IC internal short
Graphics to UMA (DIS to UMA) conversion technique

courseContent.title: Module 13: DC Power Supply & Short Circuit Detection
courseContent.description: Practical usage of DC power supply, power injection methods, and short circuit tracing techniques.
topics: DC power supply introduction and usage

courseContent.title: Module 14: DSO/CRO Advanced Testing Techniques
courseContent.description: Advanced oscilloscope settings, waveform analysis, trigger modes, and signal integrity testing for accurate motherboard diagnostics.
topics: Noise and distortion detection using DSO

careerOutcomes: Certificate of Completion from Laptian Technical Institute upon successful course completion
Increased technical confidence in diagnostics and troubleshooting
Career advancement toward becoming a chip-level repair expert

futureSection.heading: Prepare Yourself for the <highlight>Future</highlight> of Laptop Repair
futureSection.description: The laptop repair industry is evolving rapidly with new technologies, components, and repair techniques emerging constantly. Our training program keeps pace with these changes, ensuring you're always ahead of the curve.
futureSection.closingText: With the increasing complexity of modern laptops and the growing demand for skilled technicians, now is the perfect time to invest in your laptop repair career. Join Laptian Technical Institute and become part of a thriving community of successful repair professionals.

items.title: Desktop Repair Training
items.description: Learn fundamental laptop repair skills including hardware troubleshooting, component replacement, and basic diagnostics.
items.detailedDescription: Our Basic Laptop Repair course is designed to provide you with a solid foundation in laptop repair and maintenance. This beginner-friendly program covers essential hardware components, common issues, and practical repair techniques that will enable you to diagnose and fix typical laptop problems confidently.
whoShouldEnroll: Complete beginners with no prior technical experience
Students looking to start a career in laptop repair
Small business owners wanting to handle basic IT issues
Anyone interested in understanding how laptops work
Professionals seeking a career change into tech services
items.practicalTraining: Our hands-on approach ensures you work with real laptops from day one. You'll disassemble and reassemble various laptop models, diagnose actual hardware failures, and perform component replacements under expert supervision. This practical experience builds muscle memory and confidence that theoretical learning alone cannot provide.

curriculum.title: Laptop Architecture & Components
curriculum.description: Understand the building blocks of a desktop computer and how they work together.

curriculum.title: Power System Troubleshooting
curriculum.description: Diagnose and fix common power supply and startup issues.

curriculum.title: Display & Input Devices
curriculum.description: Repair display, keyboard, and touchpad related problems.

curriculum.title: Storage & Memory
curriculum.description: Manage storage devices and perform RAM upgrades.

curriculum.title: OS & Software
curriculum.description: Install operating systems and troubleshoot common software issues.

curriculum.title: Diagnostics & Best Practices
curriculum.description: Use professional diagnostic tools and follow industry repair standards.

courseContent.title: Week 1: Desktop Fundamentals
courseContent.description: Get hands-on with desktop hardware — learn to assemble, disassemble, and perform essential soldering work.

courseContent.title: Week 2: Repair & Diagnostics
courseContent.description: Apply your skills to real-world repair scenarios covering BIOS, circuit tracing, and OS-level troubleshooting.
topics: RAM, VRM, PCH tracing and troubleshooting

careerOutcomes: Start as a Junior Laptop Technician

futureSection.heading: Prepare Yourself for the <highlight>Future</highlight> of Desktop Repair
futureSection.description: Desktop repair skills form the essential foundation for all computer repair careers. Master these fundamentals and you'll be ready to take on any hardware challenge.
futureSection.closingText: Desktop repair is the first step toward becoming a complete hardware professional. Build a strong foundation and unlock opportunities across the entire computer repair industry.

items.title: Laptop Chip Level Repair Training
items.description: Master complex chip-level repairs, circuit analysis, and component-level troubleshooting techniques.
highlights: Laptop Motherboard Fundamentals & Power Supply Sequence
VRM, RAM, PCH & Clock, Fan, USB, LVDS, HDMI, Audio Circuits
items.detailedDescription: Take your skills to the next level with our Advanced Motherboard Repair course. This intensive program delves deep into component-level troubleshooting, circuit board analysis, and micro-soldering techniques. You'll learn to diagnose and repair complex motherboard issues that most technicians consider irreparable.
whoShouldEnroll: Technicians with basic laptop repair experience
Graduates of our Basic Laptop Repair course
Anyone looking to master high-value repair skills
items.practicalTraining: Work with advanced diagnostic equipment including oscilloscopes, multimeters, and thermal cameras. You'll practice BGA reballing, micro-soldering on actual motherboards, and learn to read schematics like a pro. Our experienced instructors guide you through real-world repair scenarios that you'll encounter in professional settings.

curriculum.title: Motherboard Architecture
curriculum.description: Deep dive into laptop motherboard layouts, power delivery, and circuit theory.

curriculum.title: Schematic Reading & Diagnostics
curriculum.description: Read schematics and perform component-level fault diagnosis.

curriculum.title: Micro-Soldering & BGA
curriculum.description: Professional soldering techniques including BGA reballing and rework.

curriculum.title: CPU & GPU Troubleshooting
curriculum.description: Diagnose and repair processor and graphics-related failures.

curriculum.title: BIOS & EC Repair
curriculum.description: BIOS programming, recovery, and embedded controller repair.

curriculum.title: Advanced Tools & Workshop Setup
curriculum.description: Master advanced testing equipment and set up a professional repair workshop.

courseContent.title: Week 1-2: Motherboard Fundamentals
courseContent.description: Understand laptop motherboard architecture from the ground up, including power delivery and charging circuits.
topics: Power supply sequence and DC-IN circuits

courseContent.title: Week 3-4: Circuit-Level Diagnostics
courseContent.description: Master circuit-level diagnostics across all major laptop subsystems using professional-grade tools.
topics: VRM, RAM, PCH and clock circuits
Fan, USB, LVDS, HDMI, and audio circuits

courseContent.title: Week 5-6: Advanced Repair Techniques
courseContent.description: Learn advanced repair skills including micro-soldering, BIOS programming, and physical fabrication repair.
topics: Professional workshop setup and best practices

futureSection.heading: Prepare Yourself for the <highlight>Future</highlight> of Chip-Level Repair
futureSection.description: Chip-level repair is the most in-demand and highest-paying skill in the electronics repair industry. Stay ahead with cutting-edge techniques and advanced diagnostic expertise.
futureSection.closingText: As laptops become more complex, chip-level technicians are in higher demand than ever. Position yourself as an expert and command premium rates in this growing field.
